title: Measuring Robustness
concept: Robustness is a model's ability to maintain high accuracy when tested on noisy or shifted out-of-distribution data.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the Sim2Real gap?

options.text: The performance drop when a model trained in simulation is deployed in the real world
The coordinate conversion mismatch between YOLO and COCO datasets
The time delay between image loading and GPU inference

exercises.explain: Sim2Real refers to the domain shift between virtual training worlds and physical reality.

cards.title: The Stress Test
cards.body: To test if a bridge is safe, you don't just measure how it holds up on a calm, sunny day. You test it against high winds, earthquakes, and heavy traffic. To test if an AI model is safe, we must stress-test its robustness against noise and shift.

cards.title: Model Robustness
cards.body: Model Robustness measures how well a neural network generalizes and maintains its performance when tested on Out-of-Distribution (OOD) data, corrupted inputs (noise, blur), or adversarial perturbations.

cards.title: The All-Weather Athlete
cards.body: A standard model is like a runner who only trains on an indoor track. They are fast, but get easily winded in mud, rain, or altitude. A robust model is like an all-weather trail runner who maintains speed on rocks, mud, and steep slopes.

cards.title: Robustness Benchmarks
cards.body: Researchers use datasets like **ImageNet-C** (Corrupted) to test models. It contains the standard ImageNet test images altered with 15 types of corruptions: noise (Gaussian, impulse), blur (defocus, motion), weather (snow, frost), and digital filters.

cards.title: Accuracy does not equal robustness
cards.body: You might think the model with the highest clean accuracy score is naturally the most robust. In reality, models can achieve 99% accuracy by overfitting to tiny, clean texture details. When tested on blurry images, their accuracy collapses faster than simpler models.

cards.title: Standard vs. Robust Evaluation
cards.body: How we test a model's limits:

left.text:
Evaluates on clean test set.
Verifies default performance.
Hides vulnerability to noise.

right.text:
Evaluates on corrupted data.
Verifies safety limits.
Guarantees real-world reliability.

exercises.prompt: What does a model's robustness measure?
exercises.explain: Robustness is the capacity of a model to withstand variations, noise, and shifts in test data.

options.text: Its ability to maintain performance on noisy, corrupted, or shifted out-of-distribution inputs
The speed at which it calculates gradients during the backward pass
The number of parameters in the model architecture
The quality of annotations in the training set

exercises.prompt: True or False: ImageNet-C is a benchmark dataset designed to evaluate model accuracy on clean, high-resolution studio photos.
exercises.explain: False. ImageNet-C is specifically modified with 15 types of corruptions (blur, noise, weather) to test model robustness.
Distorted inputs differ from clean training images, representing out-of-distribution test data.
exercises.sentence: Testing models on distorted inputs like motion blur or snow checks if they can handle ___ data.

exercises.prompt: Which model is more robust based on these test results?
exercises.explain: Model A is highly robust because it retains most of its performance when faced with noisy data, whereas Model B collapses.

options.text: Model A: Scores 92% on clean data and maintains 85% on heavily corrupted data
Model B: Scores 96% on clean data but drops to 40% on corrupted data
Model C: Scores 50% on clean data and 50% on corrupted data

exercises.prompt: You are building a vision model for a surgical robot. Model A has 98% accuracy on clean surgical video, but drops to 70% under camera glare. Model B has 94% clean accuracy, but maintains 90% accuracy under glare. Which model should you deploy?

options.text: Model B, because in high-stakes environments, robustness under visual distortions is more critical than a minor edge in clean performance.
Model A, because 98% is a higher number and clean performance is the only official metric.
Neither; surgical robots must only be operated in total darkness.
Model A, but add code to speed up the GPU frame rate to compensate.

exercises.explain: Robustness under stress (glare) prevents catastrophic failures in production, making Model B safer for high-stakes medical tasks.
